column1: C&P-2023-contract review-02-03,"2023","Procure to Pay 2023-01","2","Procure to Pay-2023-02","Procure to Pay","Ineffective controls over contract management","<table width=""934"" style=""width&#58
Internal Audit Plan for the year 2023, the Internal Audit Office conducted a
review of United Software (contract_2021 and contract 3389) under the Procure
to Pay assignment. The audit findings are as follows&#58

column10: It was observed that the contractors

column1: have access to both the staging and production environments on AWS. This
raises concerns regarding potential security risks and the need for proper

column3: it was discovered that a user, likely

column1: affiliated with United Software, has access to a GitHub repository containing
the source code of Dubai Future Academy. This presents a high risk and
highlights the importance of reviewing and restricting access permissions.
Future Foundation (DFF) has the right to conduct audits of all projects
handled by United Software. However, it was found that no audit has been
conducted since the initiation of the original contract. This raises concerns

column10: There is a lack of profile

column1: segregation between third-party employees and DFF employees. The provided
list, in the form of a screenshot from the IT team, does not clearly specify
the type of permissions granted, the level of access rights, or the specific
DFF systems to which access is granted. This ambiguity poses a potential
security risk and calls for improved access management practices.

column10: Upon examining Jira, it was observed

column1: that there is inadequate time tracking for most of the projects handled by
United Software. This lack of tracking hinders accurate monitoring of project
progress, resource allocation, and accountability.

column10: As per Schedule 5, Section C of the

column1: contract, each invoice should include specific details such as the order
number, specified services provided, deliverables provided, and the
calculated amount in accordance with the agreement and any orders. However,
upon reviewing a sample of invoices, it was noted that the specified services
provided, and any deliverables provided were missing. This non-compliance
with the invoicing requirements calls for improved documentation and

and decisions. By overstepping the prescribed authority, the Director of IT
acted beyond their designated scope, potentially leading to issues related to
